    for word in sports_keywords:

            scores["Sports"] += 1
    for word in business_keywords:
        if word in text:
            scores["Business"] += 1
    for word in world_keywords:
        if word in text:
            scores["World"] += 1
            
    # Heuristics for disambiguation
    if "game" in text:
        if any(w in text for w in ["video", "console", "nintendo", "sony", "xbox", "computer", "online", "software"]):

        elif any(w in text for w in ["league", "season", "team", "coach", "ball", "score", "win", "loss"]):

    if "apple" in text:
        if any(w in text for w in ["stock", "market", "share", "profit"]):
            scores["Business"] += 1
        else:
            scores["Sci/Tech"] += 1
            
    # Return category with max score
    if max(scores.values()) == 0:
        return "Unknown"
    
    return max(scores, key=scores.get)

# Re-use classification logic or just print for manual check if list is small (it is 17)

# To save space/time, I will just print title and description for ALL articles, 
# but specifically highlighting the ones my classifier thought were Tech.
# Or better: I will print title and description for ALL articles in the list (111 items) 
# so I can inspect the relevant ones. 111 is not too many for me to read (as an LLM).
# But the output limit is 10k chars. 111 articles * ~200 chars = 22k chars. Too big.
# I will filter and print only those that contain "science", "tech", "computer", "web", "microsoft", "google", "space", "game", "software", "internet" etc in title or description.

# Or better, just print the 17 found.

tech_keywords = [
    "science", "technology", "tech", "computer", "software", "hardware", "internet", "web", 
    "online", "digital", "mobile", "phone", "wireless", "broadband", "satellite", "space", 
    "nasa", "astronomy", "biology", "physics", "genetics", "medical research", "robot", 
    "artificial intelligence", "gadget", "device", "chip", "processor", "microsoft", "apple", 
    "google", "intel", "linux", "windows", "browser", "search engine", "video game", "gaming", 
    "console", "hacker", "virus", "cyber", "security", "network", "telecom", "nintendo", 
    "sony", "xbox", "playstation", "wii", "ipod", "mp3", "dvd", "hdtv", "camera", "ibm", 
    "yahoo", "ebay", "amazon", "facebook", "twitter", "myspace", "youtube", "blog", "wiki",
    "gameboy", "ds", "psp", "smartphone", "laptop", "desktop", "server", "data", "database",
    "algorithm", "code", "program", "developer", "engineer", "scientist", "researcher", 
    "laboratory", "experiment", "study", "discovery", "innovation", "invention", "stem cell",
    "clone", "dna", "genome", "mars", "moon", "galaxy", "solar system", "universe", "planet",
    "asteroid", "comet", "meteor", "telescope", "microscope", "nanotech", "biotech",
    "fuel cell", "hybrid", "electric car"
]

# I will use a simplified check to print candidates.
candidates = []
for a in articles:
    text = (a['title'] + " " + a['description']).lower()
    # Check if ANY tech keyword is in text
    if any(k in text for k in tech_keywords):
        candidates.append({"title": a['title'], "desc": a['description']})

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(candidates))
